items.title: Tottenham latest: Striker exit confirmed
Elliot Anderson’s transfer fee makes me look like a £50m bargain – Man City are making a problem for themselves
items.summary: ELLIOT ANDERSON is a great player, who had a good season at Nottingham Forest and is doing well at this World Cup. But there will be expectations of him now. Manchester City are there to win leagues. Elliot needs to get his head round that as quickly as possible — that he’s going to be...

items.title: England legend names TWO huge changes Thomas Tuchel must make to Three Lions XI for Panama World Cup clash
items.summary: THOMAS TUCHEL has been urged to make TWO big changes to his England side for Saturday’s World Cup clash with Panama. Three Lions legend Teddy Sheringham believes Manchester United forward Marcus Rashford deserves a recall after his impact off the bench, while Kobbie Mainoo should be handed a chance

items.title: Sri Lanka keep Women's T20 World Cup semi-finals hopes alive with Scotland win
items.summary: Sri Lanka kept alive their slim hopes of making the Women's T20 World Cup semi-finals for the first time after a dramatic last-over win over Scotland at Old Trafford.

items.title: England latest: 'Anderson could miss Panama game'
James injury concerns grow but Rice back in training
items.summary: Reece James could miss the start of the World Cup knockout stage if England progress as he recovers from injury.

items.title: Brilliant Dembele hat-trick helps France see off Norway to top group
items.summary: Ousmane Dembele's first-half hat-trick helps France soundly defeat a Norway side without Erling Haaland 4-1 to top Group I at the World Cup.

items.title: Gueye scores 'venomous' double off the Senegal bench
items.summary: Senegal midfielder Pape Gueye produced two moments of quality in front of goal soon after coming off the bench, scoring an impressive double against Iraq in their Group I battle to help fire his side into the last 32.

items.title: Dembélé hat-trick fires France past Norway to seal top spot in World Cup group
items.summary: Does anyone at the World Cup have the defensive strength to deny this hyper-mobile, supremely varied French attack? items.title: Senegal salvage hopes of World Cup last-32 place with rout of 10-man Iraq
items.summary: Substitute Pape Gueye scored a pair of sensational second-half goals from long range, and Senegal got a much-needed boost to their goal difference with a 5-0 victory over 10-man Iraq in their Group I finale on Friday. items.summary: FORGET the battle of the big two for the Golden Boot. That didn’t happen. Instead, it was the man with the Ballon d’Or on his sideboard who got everyone talking in Boston. items.title: Chelsea close in on Lacroix - Saturday's gossip
items.summary: Chelsea close in on Crystal Palace centre-back Maxence Lacroix, Newcastle United add Borussia Dortmund midfielder Felix Nmecha to their shortlist, while three Premier League clubs are chasing Uruguay left-back Maxi Araujo.

items.title: Premier League giants receive whopping £570,000 fine for major rule breach rule in NINE games last season
items.summary: ASTON VILLA have been hit with a whopping £570,000 fine by the Premier League after repeatedly breaching one specific rule. items.title: Senegal thrash Iraq to close on WC knockout stages - and potential meeting with England
items.summary: Senegal gave themselves the best chance of finishing as one of the World Cup's top eight third-placed teams as they thumped five past 10-man Iraq.

items.title: Capsey urges England to continue winning momentum at T20 World Cup
items.summary: All-rounder Alice Capsey has urged England to continue their winning momentum against New Zealand at The Oval ahead of semi-finals of the T20 World Cup.

items.title: What each team need to reach World Cup knockouts
items.summary: With the final round of group fixtures nearing a conclusion the path to the World Cup final on 19 July is starting to take shape.

items.title: Senegal thrash ten-man Iraq to boost hopes of reaching last 32
items.summary: Ilimand Ndiaye and Ismaila Sarr produce a goal and an assist each as Senegal dismantle ten-man Iraq 5-0 in their final FIFA World Cup Group I match at Toronto Stadium, to put themselves in contention of qualifying for the round of 32 as one of the top eight third-placed teams.

items.title: 17 passes, all 11 players - Dembele scores 'magic' goal for France
items.summary: Every member of France's starting XI gets involved as a 17-pass move is put away by Ousmane Dembele to seal his hat-trick in France's 4-1 win over Norway in their final FIFA World Cup Group I match at Boston Stadium.

items.title: Dembele's 'magic' first-half hat-trick
items.summary: France forward Ousmane Dembele stuns Norway with an incredible hat-trick in the opening 32 minutes of their Group I World Cup clash in Boston.

items.title: Yimvert Berroteran dead aged 18: Highly-rated footballer dies after being trapped under rubble in devastating earthquake
items.summary: FOOTBALLER Yimvert Berroteran has tragically died aged 18 after being trapped under the rubble during the Venezuelan earthquakes.
